chapter_title.en: Chapter 92 – The perfume for the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/312)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/312)
text: لَا بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تَشَمَّ الْإِذْخِرَ وَ الْقَيْصُومَ وَ الْخُزَامَى وَ الشِّيحَ وَ أَشْبَاهَهُ وَ أَنْتَ مُحْرِمٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said,
‘There is no problem with it if you were to smell the lemon-grass, and the Achilles (plant), and the lavender; and wormwood, and whatever resembles it, while you are in Ihraam’.
en.sarwar: 14. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah ibn Hilal from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who in the state of Ihram, while sleeping, comes in contact with perfumes, and he does not know about it. He (the Imam) said, ‘He must wash it and there is nothing on him.’ I asked him (the Imam) about the case of one not in the state of Ihram applying perfume to one who is in the state of Ihram who does not know what is on him. He (the Imam) said, ‘He also must wash it clean and there is nothing on him but he must remain careful.”’
